member.name: Daisy Cooper
member.synopsis: Daisy Cooper is the Liberal Democrat MP for St Albans , and has been an MP continually since 12 December 2019. She currently undertakes the role of Liberal Democrat Spokesperson (Treasury). In addition, she is Deputy Leader, Liberal Democrats.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Local Government: Hertfordshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, if he will announce the decision on the number of unitary authorities to be created in Hertfordshire as a result of Local Government Re-organisation before the Summer Recess.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As per our published timetable, a decision is expected on which proposals will be implemented before the summer recess.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Local Government: Hertfordshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, if they will announce their decision on the number of unitary authorities to be created in Hertfordshire.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As per our published timetable, a decision is expected on which proposals will be implemented before the summer recess.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Individual Savings Accounts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what representations she has received from the investment management industry on changes regarding money market funds in stocks and shares ISAs.
writtenQuestions.answerText: At Autumn Budget 2025, the Government announced a reduction in the Cash ISA limit for those under 65, to encourage retail investment and drive better returns for savers. Rules are needed to protect the integrity of the Government’s objectives and ensure t...

writtenQuestions.heading: Individual Savings Accounts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what information her Department holds on the extent to which money market funds within stocks and shares ISAs are used as temporary holding vehicles rather than long-term cash substitutes.
writtenQuestions.answerText: At Autumn Budget 2025, the Government announced a reduction in the Cash ISA limit for those under 65, to encourage retail investment and drive better returns for savers. Rules are needed to protect the integrity of the Government’s objectives and ensure t...

writtenQuestions.heading: Revenue and Customs: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of the extent of the use of Large Language Models in providing inaccurate advice to HMRC customers relating to (a) self-assessment and (b) corporation tax returns.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to helping taxpayers get their tax right. While the use of external Large Language Models can be helpful, they may not always provide accurate tax advice and HMRC has no control over the way these models are trained. HMRC there...

writtenQuestions.heading: Revenue and Customs: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of the extent of the use of Large Language Models in cases of (a) tax evasion and (b) fraud.
writtenQuestions.answerText: HMRC recognises the rapid growth in the use of large language models (LLMs) and other generative AI tools and their potential to affect tax compliance. These technologies are widely accessible and are increasingly used by taxpayers and the wider general p...

writtenQuestions.heading: Railway Stations: CCTV
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the Answer of 20 May to Question 510 on Railway Stations: CCTV, whether details have been made available by Network Rail on whether bike thefts will be included in the assessment of railway stations
writtenQuestions.answerText: Further to the answer of 20 May 2026 to Question 510, this work remains in progress by Network Rail, who are delivering the project on behalf of the rail industry. The requested details will be available in 2027/28.

writtenQuestions.heading: Rivers: Sewage
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what assessment she has made of the potential merits of implementing the measures in the Chalk Streams (Sewerage Investment) Bill.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government is committed to the protection and restoration of our cherished chalk streams. We are already taking action to hold water companies and other polluters to account through the Water (Special Measures) Act 2025 and water companies are requir...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: National Savings and Investments: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment her Department has made of adequacy of the time taken for National Savings and Investments to action a request for a valuation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: National Savings and Investment (NS&I) customers with an online account can log in and see the value of their investments instantly. Alternatively, NS&I customers can phone the customer service team for a valuation, which can be provided during th...

writtenQuestions.heading: Antisemitism: Curriculum
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what assessment she has made of the potential merits of including content on contemporary antisemitism within the existing mandatory Holocaust material in the school curriculum.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The purpose of Holocaust education in history is to teach about why and how the Holocaust happened. This will include teaching about antisemitism in this historical context due to the centrality of antisemitism to the Nazi’s ideology. Such teaching can pr...

writtenQuestions.heading: Diethylstilbestrol
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what training healthcare professionals are given on the impacts of diethylstilbestrol.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I apologise and express my sincere sympathy to all those affected by exposure to diethylstilbestrol (DES), including those exposed in utero and their families.The Government’s focus is on carefully considering the available evidence and determining the mo...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Diethylstilbestrol: Side Effects
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if he would hold a public inquiry into the effects of diethylstilbestrol.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I apologise and express my sincere sympathy to all those affected by exposure to diethylstilbestrol (DES), including those exposed in utero and their families.The Government’s focus is on carefully considering the available evidence and determining the mo...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: MBR Acres: Dogs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, whether she has received reports on welfare conditions for beagle puppies at MBR Acres, in the context of the current heatwave.
writtenQuestions.answerText: ASRU maintains regular engagement with MBR Acres as part of its ongoing oversight of establishments licenced under the Animals (Scientific Procedures) Act 1986 (ASPA). This includes updates on operational matters and environmental conditions, including te...

writtenQuestions.heading: Water Supply: Hertfordshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether she has had discussions with Affinity Water on the reduction of water pressure to residents of Park Street village, Hertfordshire.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Defra Ministers and officials engage regularly with the water sector, including specific companies, on strategic policy issues. The Government expects water companies to keep affected customers informed and to take all appropriate steps to minimise disrup...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Building Societies: Meetings
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of the potential implications for her Department's regulatory policies of building societies using electronic quick vote systems to approve board recommendations at AGMs.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government is committed to supporting the growth and long-term success of the mutual sector, including building societies, in line with its manifesto commitment to double the size of the mutual and co‑operative sector. The Government recognises the i...

writtenQuestions.heading: Meningitis: Vaccination
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether he has made an assessment of the potential merits of expanding eligibility for the MenB vaccination programme to students aged 26 and older.
writtenQuestions.answerText: On 12 June 2026, we announced a one-off, time-limited meningococcal group B (MenB) immunisation programme to protect those at highest immediate risk of MenB disease this autumn.People currently in the Year 13 age group in England and Wales, those born fro...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Special Educational Needs: Speech and Language Disorders
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps she is taking to help improve the support available for children and young people with Auditory Processing Disorder as part of the planned SEND reforms.
writtenQuestions.answerText: All children and young people with special educational needs and disabilities, including those with Auditory Processing Disorder, deserve the best possible educational experience. The department will develop National Inclusion Standards that set out evide...

writtenQuestions.heading: Special Educational Needs: Speech and Language Disorders
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether she will make an assessment of the adequacy of the current Education, Health and Care Plan system in supporting children and young people with Auditory Processing Disorder.
writtenQuestions.answerText: All children and young people with special educational needs and disabilities, including those with Auditory Processing Disorder, deserve the best possible educational experience. The department will develop National Inclusion Standards that set out evide...

writtenQuestions.heading: Emergency Services: Monuments
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, whether he plans to erect a National Emergency Services Memorial.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There are currently no plans to erect a National Emergency Services Memorial.

writtenQuestions.heading: Psychiatric Hospitals: Hertfordshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the adequacy of acute mental health inpatient places in Hertfordshire.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Since the pandemic, mental health services in Hertfordshire have faced sustained pressure, including a reliance on out‑of‑area beds, reflecting national trends. A 2023/24 review found local inpatient capacity was significantly below comparable areas.In re...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
